```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
theta <- sample(c(3, 5, 8, 10), 1)
n     <- sample(c(10, 15, 20, 25), 1)
x_max <- round(theta * runif(1, 0.7, 0.99), 4)

## IC para theta de U(0,theta):
## X_{(n)}/theta ~ Beta(n,1), F(y) = y^n
## P(X_{(n)}/theta >= c) = 0.05 => 1 - c^n = 0.05 => c = (0.05)^{1/n}
## Limite superior: x_max / (0.05)^{1/n}
correct <- round(x_max / (0.05)^(1 / n), 4)
w1 <- round(x_max * n / (n - 1), 4)
w2 <- round(x_max + 1 / n, 4)
w3 <- round(x_max / (1 - 0.95^(1 / n)), 4)
w4 <- round(x_max * (1 + 0.05 / n), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para $X_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} U(0,\theta)$, o maximo $X_{(n)} = `r x_max`$ e suficiente para $\theta$. Sabe-se que $Y_n = X_{(n)}/\theta \sim \text{Beta}(n,1)$, com $F_{Y_n}(y) = y^n$. Um IC 95% para $\theta$ baseado em $X_{(n)}$ (com $n = `r n`$) tem limite superior:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$P(X_{(n)}/\theta \geq c) = 1 - c^n = 0{,}05 \Rightarrow c = (0{,}05)^{1/n}$.

O IC 95% e $\left(X_{(n)},\; X_{(n)}/(0{,}05)^{1/n}\right)$. O limite superior e $`r x_max` / (0{,}05)^{1/`r n`} = `r correct`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S2Q6_IC_uniforme_maximo
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
